document.title: FUJIFILM Tomosynthesis option for FDR AcSelerate Stationary X-ray System - 510(k) K121499
document.documentType: 510(k) Summary and FDA substantial equivalence letter
productModels: FUJIFILM Tomosynthesis option for FDR AcSelerate Stationary X-ray System
authorityOrJurisdiction: Center for Devices and Radiological Health
document.documentDate: 510(k) Summary Date: October 29, 2012; FDA letter: November 20, 2012

sourceSummary.oneLine: FDA determined FUJIFILM Tomosynthesis option for FDR AcSelerate Stationary X-ray System, K121499, substantially equivalent for tomographic imaging use.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory wiki facts for FDA 510(k) clearance, indications for use, classification, standards, predicate devices, and device description.
keyPoints: Trade/device name is FUJIFILM Tomosynthesis option for FDR AcSelerate Stationary X-ray System.
The device is intended to acquire tomographic images of human anatomy and synthesize tomographic slices from a single tomographic sweep.
The indications state it is not intended for mammographic applications.
FDA listed Regulation Number 21 CFR 892.1740, Regulation Name Tomographic x-ray system, Regulatory Class II, and Product Code IZF.
FDA determined the device substantially equivalent and stated it may be marketed subject to general controls provisions of the Act.

evidence.claim: FDA issued the substantial equivalence letter on November 20, 2012.
The submission was dated October 29, 2012 and received November 1, 2012.
The device is regulated under 21 CFR 892.1740 as a tomographic x-ray system.
evidence.evidenceText: Regulation Number: 21 CFR 892.1740 Regulation Name: Tomographic x-ray system

evidence.claim: The regulatory class is Class II.
The indications for use are to acquire tomographic images of human anatomy and to be used with Fujifilm's DR X-ray systems.
evidence.evidenceText: Fujifilm's Tomosynthesis option is intended to acquire tomographic images of human anatomy and to be used with Fujifilm's DR X-ray systems.

evidence.claim: Tomosynthesis synthesizes tomographic slices from a single tomographic sweep.
evidence.evidenceText: Tomosynthesis is used to synthesize tomographic slices from a single tomographic sweep.

evidence.claim: The device is not intended for mammographic applications.
evidence.evidenceText: It is not intended for mammographic applications.

evidence.claim: FDA determined the device substantially equivalent to legally marketed predicate devices.
evidence.evidenceText: have determined the device is substantially equivalent

evidence.claim: The device may be marketed subject to general controls provisions of the Act.
evidence.evidenceText: You may, therefore, market the device, subject to the general controls provisions of the Act.

evidence.claim: Predicate devices listed include Revolutionar XR/d with Tomosynthesis K051967 and FDR AcSelerate Stationary X-ray System K093427.
evidence.evidenceText: Revolutionar XR/d with Tomnosynthesis K051967E FDR AcSelerate Stationar X-ray Sstemn K093427

evidence.claim: The proposed device conforms to listed voluntary standards including IEC 60601 series, IEC 60825-1, UL 60601-1, and DICOM.
evidence.evidenceText: The proposed device conforms to the following voluntary standards as applicable to the system.

evidence.claim: Bench testing and software verification and validation testing were completed with satisfactory results.
evidence.evidenceText: Various bench testing in addition to software verification and validation testing to evaluate the Tomosynthesis performance were completed and the results were satisfactory.

reviewFlags.reason: OCR quality issues are present, including 'Class 11' for Class II, 'lEG' for IEC, misspelled predicate names, and broken URLs.
Source contains both a 510(k) Summary dated October 29, 2012 and an FDA letter dated November 20, 2012; both dates are retained.
The standards section includes apparent OCR or source inconsistency for 'IEC 60601-2-25' while describing 'Part 2-28'.
No revision or version number was present in the source text.
